info.name: Rachqalik API
info.description:
Complete REST API for the Rachqalik sleep platform.

Base URL: http://localhost:5000

---

**Authentication**
Most endpoints require a Bearer token in the Authorization header.
Run "Login" or "Admin Login" first — the token is saved automatically as a collection variable.

**Workflow to get started:**
1. Run "Login" to set `token` and `userId`
2. Run "Admin Login" to set `adminToken`
3. Run "Get All Products" to set `productId`
4. Run "Create Order" to set `orderId`

**Plans and access:**
- `free` users have a 15-day trial to use sleep tracking
- `premium` users have unlimited access
- `admin` role required for product and order management

request.description: Returns 200 if the API server is running and reachable. No authentication required. Use this to confirm the server is up before running other requests.

item.name: Auth
item.description:
User registration, login, profile management, plan upgrade, and password reset.

All protected endpoints require: `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

request.description:
Creates a new user account.

**Validation rules:**
- `name`: minimum 2 characters
- `email`: must be a valid email format, must be unique
- `password`: minimum 8 characters

New accounts start on the `free` plan with a 15-day sleep tracking trial.

Returns a JWT token valid for 7 days.

exec: pm.test('Response has token and user', function () {

response.name: 400 Validation Error

request.description:
Authenticates a user and returns a JWT token.

On success, the test script automatically saves:
- `token` — used by all protected user endpoints
- `userId` — used for order and sleep history requests

The token is valid for 7 days. Passwords are compared using bcrypt.

Logs in with an admin account.

On success, saves `adminToken` automatically. Use `adminToken` for any endpoint that requires admin access (creating/deleting products, updating order status).

To create an admin account locally, run:
```
node server/config/createAdmin.js
```
Default credentials: admin@example.com / admin1234

Returns the profile of the currently authenticated user.

Used by the frontend on page load to verify the token is still valid and retrieve up-to-date user data (name, plan, role).

Requires: `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

Updates the name and/or password of the authenticated user.

Both fields are optional — send only what you want to change.
- Omit `name` to keep the current name
- Omit `password` to keep the current password

At least one field must be provided.

**Validation:**
- `name`: minimum 2 characters
- `password`: minimum 8 characters (will be hashed before saving)

Requires: `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

response.name: 400 Nothing to Update

request.description:
Upgrades the authenticated user's plan from `free` to `premium`.

This endpoint has no request body — simply call it with a valid token.
In production this would be gated behind a payment confirmation step.

Once upgraded, the 15-day trial limit is removed and sleep tracking is available indefinitely.

Returns 400 if the user is already on the premium plan.

Requires: `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

Resets a user's password directly by email.

No authentication required. In a production environment this would trigger an email with a time-limited reset link. For local development it resets the password immediately.

The response does not reveal whether the email exists in the database — both found and not-found return 200 to prevent user enumeration.

**Validation:**
- `email`: must be a valid email format
- `newPassword`: minimum 8 characters

response.name: 200 OK — Email Not Found (no leak)
400 Weak Password

item.name: Products
item.description:
Product catalog management.

- `GET /` and `GET /:id` are public — no token required
- `POST /` and `DELETE /:id` require admin access (`adminToken`)

request.description:
Returns the full product catalog sorted by creation date (newest first).

No authentication required. The test script saves the ID of the first returned product as `productId` for use in subsequent requests.

To seed the database with sample products run:
```
npm run seed
```

exec: pm.test('Response has count and data array', function () {

request.description:
Returns a single product by its MongoDB ObjectId.

No authentication required.

Returns 400 if the ID format is invalid, and 404 if no product exists with that ID.

response.name: 200 OK

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Rachqalik Smart Pillow X",
  "price": 149.99,
  "description": "Next-generation smart pillow with AI-powered sleep analysis.",
  "image": "https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1567446537708-ac4aa75c9c28",
  "stock": 50
}

request.description:
Creates a new product. Admin access only.

**Required fields:**
- `name` (string)
- `price` (number)
- `description` (string)
- `stock` (number)

**Optional fields:**
- `image` (string URL) — defaults to empty string

Requires: `Authorization: Bearer {{adminToken}}`

Returns 403 if the token belongs to a non-admin user.

exec: pm.test('New product ID saved for delete test', function () {

response.name: 201 Created
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Product created successfully",
  "data": {
    "_id": "69e81d4b44a5a1eb74f45a17",
    "name": "Rachqalik Smart Pillow X",
    "price": 149.99,
    "description": "Next-generation smart pillow.",
    "image": "",
    "stock": 50,
    "createdAt": "2026-04-22T00:58:51.535Z",
    "updatedAt": "2026-04-22T00:58:51.535Z"
  }
}

response.name: 400 Missing Required Fields

request.description:
Permanently deletes a product by ID. Admin access only.

The deleted product data is returned in the response so you can confirm what was removed.

Returns 400 for an invalid ID format, 404 if the product does not exist, and 403 for non-admin tokens.

Requires: `Authorization: Bearer {{adminToken}}`

Note: The variable `{{newProductId}}` is auto-set by the "Create Product" test script.

response.name: 200 OK — Deleted
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Product deleted successfully",
  "data": {
    "_id": "69e81d4b44a5a1eb74f45a17",
    "name": "Rachqalik Smart Pillow X",
    "price": 149.99,
    "description": "Next-generation smart pillow.",
    "image": "",
    "stock": 50,
    "createdAt": "2026-04-22T00:58:51.535Z",
    "updatedAt": "2026-04-22T00:58:51.535Z"
  }
}

item.name: Orders
item.description:
Order creation and management.

- Creating an order validates that all products exist and have sufficient stock, then decrements stock automatically
- Users can only view their own orders
- Admins can view any user's orders and update order status

request.description:
Creates a new order for the authenticated user.

**Required fields:**
- `products` (array, min 1 item): each item must include `productId`, `name`, `quantity`, `price`
- `total` (number): total order value in currency units

**Optional fields:**
- `status` (string): defaults to `pending`. Accepted values: `pending`, `paid`, `shipped`

**Stock validation:**
Before saving, the server verifies that all `productId` values exist and that each product has enough stock. If validation passes, stock is decremented for each product in the order.

Requires: `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

response.name: 201 Created
400 Product Not Found
400 Insufficient Stock

request.description:
Returns all orders placed by a specific user, sorted newest first.

**Access control:**
- A user can only fetch their own orders (token `userId` must match the `:id` param)
- An admin can fetch orders for any user

Returns 403 if a non-admin user tries to fetch another user's orders.

Requires: `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}`

exec: pm.test('Response has count and data array', function () {

request.description:
Updates the status of an existing order. Admin access only.

**Accepted status values:**
- `pending` — order created, awaiting payment
- `paid` — payment confirmed
- `shipped` — order dispatched

Returns 400 for any value outside of those three. Returns 403 if the requester is not an admin.

Requires: `Authorization: Bearer {{adminToken}}`

response.name: 200 OK — Status Updated to Paid
400 Invalid Status

item.name: Sleep
item.description:
Sleep tracking endpoints.

All sleep endpoints require authentication and an active plan.

**Plan rules:**
- `free` users: access allowed during the first 15 days after account creation
- `premium` users: unlimited access
- Expired free users receive: `403 Free trial expired after 15 days. Upgrade to premium to continue.`

request.description:
Logs a single sleep entry for the authenticated user.

**Required fields:**
- `duration` (number): hours of sleep, e.g. `7.5`
- `quality` (string): must be one of `bad`, `average`, `good`

**Optional fields:**
- `date` (ISO 8601 string): defaults to the current timestamp if omitted

Requires: `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}` and an active plan.

exec: pm.test('Sleep data saved with correct fields', function () {

response.name: 400 Invalid Quality
403 Trial Expired

request.description:
Returns all sleep records for a specific user, sorted by date descending (most recent first).

**Access control:**
- A user can only fetch their own records
- An admin can fetch records for any user

Requires: `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}` and an active plan.

exec: pm.test('Response has count and data array', function () {

request.description:
Returns aggregated sleep statistics for a specific user.

**Response includes:**
- `totalRecords`: total number of sleep entries logged
- `avgDuration`: average sleep hours (rounded to 1 decimal)
- `minDuration`: shortest sleep session logged
- `maxDuration`: longest sleep session logged
- `qualityBreakdown`: count of `good`, `average`, and `bad` nights
- `last7Days`: full records from the past 7 days, sorted oldest to newest (for charting)

If the user has no records, returns zeroed-out stats with an empty `last7Days` array.

Requires: `Authorization: Bearer {{token}}` and an active plan.

exec: pm.test('Stats response has required fields', function () {
